title: land.gov.bd ভূমি সেবা (2026) – Complete Guide to Bangladesh Land Services Portal
metaTitle: land.gov.bd ভূমি সেবা 2026 – Full Guide to Bangladesh Land Portal
metaDescription: Complete guide to land.gov.bd ভূমি সেবা in 2026. Check khatian, RS porcha, mutation status, pay khajna, order certified copies, and use BhoomiPRO & eporcha.gov.bd.
heroIntro: land.gov.bd — Bangladesh's official ভূমি সেবা (Land Services) portal — is the single digital gateway for accessing land records, tracking mutation applications, paying land tax, and ordering certified khatian copies online. Launched by the Ministry of Land, it has transformed how citizens interact with land administration in Bangladesh. This 2026 guide covers every service available on the portal and shows you exactly how to use each one.
toc: Khatian Search Online – CS, SA, RS & BS Records
RS Porcha Check by Dag Number
Free vs Paid Services on land.gov.bd
Limitations of the Portal & When to Visit AC Land Office

sections.content:
land.gov.bd is the official digital land services portal of Bangladesh, operated by the Ministry of Land (ভূমি মন্ত্রণালয়). It provides citizens with online access to land records, mutation services, tax payment, and document ordering — without requiring repeated visits to the AC Land office (উপজেলা ভূমি অফিস).

The portal is part of Bangladesh's broader Digital Bangladesh initiative and integrates multiple sub-systems:

  • land.gov.bd — Main citizen portal for land record search and services
  • bl.land.gov.bd — BhoomiPRO: integrated land management system for administration
  • eporcha.gov.bd — Certified digital porcha (khatian copy) purchase portal
  • mutation.land.gov.bd — Dedicated mutation application tracking

Whether you are buying land, settling an inheritance dispute, or applying for a bank mortgage, land.gov.bd is your first stop for verifying land ownership in Bangladesh. A land lawyer in Uttara, Dhaka uses these portals as a starting point for all property due diligence.

sections.h2: Khatian Search Online – CS, SA, RS & BS Records
sections.content:
The khatian search function on land.gov.bd lets you view historical and current land ownership records across all four major surveys of Bangladesh.

Step-by-step to search a khatian:

  1. Go to land.gov.bd and click "খতিয়ান তথ্য" (Khatian Information) from the services menu
  2. Select the Survey Type from the dropdown: CS, SA, RS, or BS
  3. Select your Division (বিভাগ)
  4. Select your District (জেলা)
  5. Select your Upazila/Thana (উপজেলা)
  6. Select your Mouza (মৌজা) — the lowest land administrative unit
  7. Enter either the Khatian Number (খতিয়ান নম্বর) or the Owner's Name (মালিকের নাম)
  8. Click Search (খুঁজুন)

The result shows the khatian record including owner name(s), dag numbers, land area per plot, and land classification. If the mouza is not yet digitised, no result will appear — in that case, visit the AC Land office directly.

⚠️ Tip: The mouza name can be found in any existing registered deed or land tax receipt. If you don't know it, ask your local UP member or the AC Land office.

sections.h2: RS Porcha Check by Dag Number
sections.content:
The RS Porcha (Revisional Survey Plot Record) shows detailed information about a specific plot (dag number) — including its area, land classification, and all khatians that include that dag.

How to check RS Porcha by Dag Number:

  1. On land.gov.bd, select "RS পর্চা" or "Plot Information"
  2. Select Division, District, Upazila, and Mouza
  3. Enter the Dag Number (দাগ নম্বর) of the specific plot
  4. Click Search — the porcha shows area, land type, and all associated khatian numbers

Why this matters:

  • If a single dag appears in two or more khatians, it signals a potential fraudulent double-entry or ownership dispute
  • Verifying the dag number against the seller's deed prevents purchasing non-existent or misrepresented land
  • Banks require confirmed dag and khatian details before approving a mortgage against land

If you spot anomalies in the porcha data, consult a property lawyer in Dhaka before proceeding with any transaction.

Mutation (নামজারি/মিউটেশন) is the process of updating the khatian to reflect a new owner after a land purchase, inheritance, or gift. In several districts, Bangladesh now allows online mutation applications through land.gov.bd.

How to apply for mutation online:

  1. Go to land.gov.bd → Select "নামজারি আবেদন" (Mutation Application)
  2. Register or log in with your mobile number (OTP verification)
  3. Fill in the applicant details: name, NID number, contact
  4. Enter the land details: Division, District, Upazila, Mouza, Dag No., Khatian No.
  5. Upload required documents (see table below)
  6. Pay the application fee online via mobile banking or card
  7. Submit and receive your mutation case number (নামজারি কেস নম্বর)
Document Required For
Registered sale deed (দলিল)Purchase-based mutation
Succession certificate / heirship documentInheritance-based mutation
Applicant's NID copyAll applications
Previous khatian copy (certified)All applications
Land tax payment receipt (khajna)All applications
Court decree (if mutation follows a court order)Court-ordered mutation

Online mutation is currently available in most upazila land offices in Dhaka, Chittagong, Sylhet, Rajshahi, and Khulna divisions. For other areas, applications may still need to be filed in person.

Once you have filed a mutation application and received a case number, you can track its progress at any time through land.gov.bd.

Steps to track mutation status:

  1. Go to land.gov.bd → Select "নামজারি অবস্থান" or "Mutation Status"
  2. Select Division, District, and Upazila
  3. Enter your Mutation Case Number (নামজারি কেস নম্বর)
  4. Click Search

Status stages you may see:

  • প্রাপ্ত (Received): Application lodged, awaiting assignment
  • তদন্তাধীন (Under Inspection): Field inspection by an AC Land officer scheduled or ongoing
  • আপত্তি দাখিল (Objection Filed): A third party has filed an objection — a hearing is needed
  • শুনানির তারিখ (Hearing Scheduled): Date set for both parties to appear before AC Land
  • আদেশ পাস (Order Passed): AC Land has ruled — either approving or rejecting mutation
  • খতিয়ান ইস্যু (Khatian Issued): New khatian issued in the applicant's name — mutation complete

If your status has been stuck at "Under Inspection" for more than 60 days with no update, a land lawyer in Dhaka can follow up formally with the AC Land office to unblock the process.

Annual land development tax — commonly known as khajna (খাজনা) — can now be paid online through land.gov.bd in most districts, eliminating the need to queue at the Union Land Office (ইউনিয়ন ভূমি সহকারী কার্যালয়).

How to pay khajna online:

  1. Go to land.gov.bd → Select "ভূমি উন্নয়ন কর" (Land Development Tax)
  2. Select your Division, District, Upazila, and Union
  3. Enter your Holding Number (হোল্ডিং নম্বর) or search by owner name
  4. View outstanding khajna balance
  5. Pay via bKash, Nagad, Rocket, debit/credit card, or internet banking
  6. Download or print the Digital Khajna Receipt (ডিজিটাল দাখিলা)

⚠️ Important: Keep all khajna receipts safely — they are required when filing a mutation application, selling land, or producing evidence of peaceful possession in a land dispute.

Online khajna payment is currently operational in most upazilas in the eight divisions. If your holding is not found online, visit the Union Land office and request to have your holding number registered in the digital system.

BhoomiPRO (accessible at bl.land.gov.bd) is Bangladesh's integrated digital land management system, connecting AC Land offices, Sub-Registrar offices, and the Ministry of Land into a single platform.

For citizens, BhoomiPRO provides:

  • Real-time mutation processing status linked directly to the AC Land office workflow
  • Integrated deed registration verification — deeds registered at the Sub-Registrar office are linked to BhoomiPRO
  • District-level land record management for government administrators
  • An advanced search interface for cross-referencing khatian, deed, and mutation records

BhoomiPRO is particularly useful for verifying whether a recently registered deed has been correctly reflected in the land administration system — an important check when purchasing land in Dhaka or other major cities where fraud is more common.

eporcha.gov.bd is the dedicated portal for purchasing certified digital copies of khatian records (porcha). Unlike the free viewing on land.gov.bd, a certified digital porcha from eporcha.gov.bd carries an official digital signature and is accepted for most formal purposes.

How to order a certified porcha from eporcha.gov.bd:

  1. Go to eporcha.gov.bd and register with your NID and mobile number
  2. Search for your khatian using Division, District, Upazila, Mouza, and Khatian Number
  3. Select the porcha and add to your order
  4. Pay the government fee online (typically BDT 100–300 per porcha depending on type)
  5. Download your digitally signed certified porcha immediately after payment

Acceptance of eporcha certified copies:

  • ✅ Accepted for mutation applications at most AC Land offices
  • ✅ Accepted for bank mortgage documentation in most banks
  • ✅ Accepted for Sub-Registrar office deed registration purposes
  • ⚠️ For court evidence, a physical certified copy with wet stamp from AC Land office may still be required — confirm with your lawyer

Contact Advocate Md. Shah Alam at our Uttara, Dhaka chamber for guidance on which type of certified document is required for your specific legal matter.

sections.h2: Free vs Paid Services on land.gov.bd
sections.content:
Understanding which services are free and which require payment saves time and avoids surprises:

Service Cost Notes
Khatian search & viewingFreeUnofficial — not usable in court
RS Porcha viewingFreeReference only
Mutation status trackingFreeRequires case number
Online khajna paymentActual tax amountNo extra portal fee
Online mutation applicationBDT 100–500Government application fee
Certified digital porcha (eporcha)BDT 100–300Digitally signed, formally valid
Physical certified copy (AC Land office)BDT 100–200Required for court proceedings

sections.h2: Limitations of the Portal & When to Visit AC Land Office
sections.content:
Despite significant improvements, land.gov.bd has limitations you should know:

  • Digitisation gaps: Many rural mouzas, especially in Barisal, Mymensingh, Rangpur, and Sylhet hill districts, are not yet fully digitised. Searches will return no results for these areas.
  • Delayed updates: The online system can lag weeks or months behind the physical AC Land office records. A recently completed mutation may not yet appear online.
  • Not legally authoritative: For court proceedings, banks, and formal applications, certified physical copies from the AC Land office remain the gold standard — online printouts are supplementary only.
  • Server downtime: The portal occasionally experiences downtime, especially during peak hours. Try early mornings or off-peak times.
  • Registration required for applications: While viewing is open, submitting applications requires NID-linked registration via OTP.

When you MUST visit the AC Land office in person:

  • Your mouza is not available on the online portal
  • You need a physical certified khatian copy for court submission
  • Your mutation application has been rejected and you want to appeal
  • There is an objection to your mutation that requires a hearing before the AC Land officer
  • You need to correct an error in the khatian record

A land and property lawyer in Uttara, Dhaka can represent you at the AC Land office for all these matters, saving you time and ensuring the process follows legal procedure correctly.

faqs.question: Is land.gov.bd free to use?
faqs.answer: Basic khatian and porcha viewing is free. Certified digital copies via eporcha.gov.bd cost BDT 100–300. Mutation applications have a government fee of BDT 100–500 depending on the district and land value.

faqs.question: What is the difference between land.gov.bd and eporcha.gov.bd?
faqs.answer: land.gov.bd is the main portal for searching records, tracking mutation status, and paying khajna for free. eporcha.gov.bd is a paid sub-portal specifically for purchasing digitally signed certified khatian copies that carry official validity for most formal purposes.

faqs.question: Can I use the online khatian printout in court?
faqs.answer: No. Bangladesh courts require certified physical copies with official stamps from the AC Land office or a digitally signed certified porcha from eporcha.gov.bd (acceptance varies by court). Confirm with your lawyer before submitting any land documents as evidence.

faqs.question: My mouza is not found on land.gov.bd. What should I do?
faqs.answer: Not all mouzas are fully digitised yet. If your mouza doesn't appear, you must visit the AC Land office (ভূমি অফিস) of the relevant upazila in person and request records from the physical record room.

faqs.question: How long does online mutation take after I apply?
faqs.answer: Online mutation applications are typically processed within 30–90 days depending on the upazila. The timeline includes field inspection, notice to adjoining landholders, and a hearing if anyone objects. If the AC Land office is busy, it can take longer — a land lawyer can help follow up to keep the process on track.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app for land.gov.bd?
faqs.answer: The Ministry of Land has released a mobile app called 'ভূমি সেবা' (Bhumi Seba) available on Google Play and Apple App Store. It provides the same core khatian search and khajna payment functions. However, the web portal is more complete for full mutation applications.
